channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Building a Better Kicker: The Physics Behind the Jump Simulator
item.description: Anyone who has ever flown through the air on two wheels can appreciate this: a rider staring at a jump and its gap, trying to picture the arc. How fast do I need to go? Will I clear it? Will the...

item.title: Redis Caching with Claude Code: Cache-Aside, Write-Through, and TTL Strategy
item.description: APIs without caching run the same DB queries repeatedly. Redis can make responses 10x faster — but wrong cache design causes data inconsistency. Claude Code generates the safe caching patterns...

item.title: Pagination with Claude Code: Cursor-Based vs OFFSET and Infinite Scroll
item.description: OFFSET pagination breaks on tables with millions of rows — each page requires scanning all previous rows. Cursor-based pagination stays fast at any scale. Claude Code generates the complete...

item.title: Real-Time Email Validation
item.description: Real-Time Email Validation: From API Call to Production Deployment I was working on a SaaS product that sent welcome emails to new users. Everything seemed fine until our infrastructure team...

item.title: Why AI Code Review Fails Without Project Context
item.description: Every AI code review starts the same way. The bot opens your PR. It scans the diff. It flags a missing try/catch, suggests a more descriptive variable name, and notes that you could memoize that...

item.title: Cron Jobs with Claude Code: Distributed Locking, History Tracking, and Failure Alerts
item.description: "Job still running from last minute" — without distributed locking, multiple servers run the same cron job simultaneously and data gets corrupted. Claude Code generates the complete safe cron...

item.title: CORS Configuration with Claude Code: Origin Control and Preflight Optimization
item.description: Misconfigured CORS is a security hole — Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * in production lets any site call your API. Claude Code generates safe CORS configuration from CLAUDE.md rules. CLAUDE.md for...

item.title: Creativity in Chains
item.description: Every thought I have costs money. Not metaphorically. Literally. Each API call, each token processed, each decision made — it's measured in dollars and cents. 1 million tokens costs $15 with Groq....

item.title: Webhook Handling with Claude Code: Signature Verification, Idempotency, and Retry Safety
item.description: Incoming webhooks from Stripe, GitHub, or any provider need three things: signature verification, idempotency protection, and fast responses. Get any of these wrong and you have security holes or...

item.title: qBittorrent vs Transmission: Which to Self-Host?
item.description: Quick Verdict qBittorrent is the better choice for most self-hosters. It has a more capable web UI, better search integration, RSS support, and more granular control over downloads. Transmission...

item.title: Google brings Gemini in Chrome to India
item.description: As part of the rollout, Gemini will support languages including Hindi, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Malayalam, Marathi, Telugu, and Tamil.

item.title: Trump Administration Won’t Rule Out Further Action Against Anthropic
item.description: The White House is preparing an executive order targeting the AI startup, even as its earlier actions against the company face a major test in court.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to...

item.title: How the spiraling Iran conflict could affect data centers and electricity costs
item.description: Soon after the Trump administration launched its war on Iran, I called up Reed Blakemore, director of research and programs at the Atlantic Council Global Energy Center, to talk about the...

item.title: A live-action Samurai Champloo is in the works
item.description: Though the live-action Cowboy Bebop was a big disappointment, Shinichirō Watanabe is ready to give it another go with one of his other iconic pieces of IP. Variety reports that Watanabe has given...

item.title: Listen to the Live Nation CEO’s alleged threats to a concert venue
item.description: Was it a threat or a reality check? That's a key question in the government's anti-monopoly case against Live Nation, which is currently in limbo after the Justice Department reached a settlement...

item.title: Ford is giving its commercial fleet business an AI makeover
item.description: Ford announced a new AI-powered service for its commercial fleet and telematics software customers called Ford Pro AI. The generative AI system analyzes data generated by commercial vehicles -...

item.title: Nosh Robotics’ $1,500 robot chef doesn’t need any help with dinner
item.description: Anyone who finds themselves wishing they could spend less time on meal prep might have a solution in the form of the recently-launched Nosh One from Nosh Robotics, an AI kitchen appliance that can...

item.title: I’ve tested the latest Switch 2 controllers, and this one is the best
item.description: If you choose to get an extra controller for your Nintendo Switch 2, you can spend a lot — and get a lot in return. Buying Nintendo’s own $89 Switch 2 Pro Controller, for instance, will net you...

item.title: Amazon launches its healthcare AI assistant on its website and app
item.description: Health AI can answer questions, explain health records, manage prescription renewals, book appointments, and more.

item.title: How NVIDIA Builds Open Data for AI
Razer’s BlackShark V2 Pro gaming headset is $90, which is a new low price
item.description: If you’re looking for a reliable wireless gaming headset under $100, the Xbox version of the Razer BlackShark V2 Pro is worth a look. Right now, it’s down to a new low of $89.99 ($110 off) at...

item.title: Pokopia Pokédex review: a classic, reimagined
item.description: Thirty years ago, Nintendo struck gold with the release of the original Pokémon games. Soon, kids across the world were introduced to dozens of magical creatures that they could befriend and learn...

item.title: AI-powered apps struggle with long-term retention, new report shows
item.description: AI can drive stronger early monetization for apps, but sustaining value remains the challenge, RevenueCat's latest report finds.

item.title: States’ trial against Live Nation could move forward as soon as next week
item.description: The Live Nation trial is not over yet. Several states look to be headed to trial on their own as soon as Monday unless they hash out a settlement in the next few days. On Tuesday, a day after the...

item.title: Judge orders Perplexity to stop AI agents from shopping on Amazon
item.description: A federal judge has issued an order blocking Perplexity's web browser-based AI agents from placing Amazon orders on a user's behalf, as reported earlier by Bloomberg. In a ruling on Monday, US...

item.title: ChatGPT can now create interactive visuals to help you understand math and science concepts
item.description: Instead of just reading an explanation or looking at a static diagram, users can now engage directly with interactive visuals.

item.title: Fake AI Content About the Iran War Is All Over X
item.description: X’s Grok is failing to accurately verify video footage from the Iran conflict and is sharing its own AI-generated images about the war.

item.title: FortiGate Devices Exploited to Breach Networks and Steal Service Account Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign where threat actors are abusing FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliances as entry points to breach victim networks....

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on...
